title: US Consumer Confidence (April)
desc: Conference Board consumer confidence release. A weak reading adds to recession fears and pressures risk assets including BTC. Expected to remain soft given tariff uncertainty and the Iran conflict overhang.

title: US PMI Flash Estimates (April)
desc: S&P Global Manufacturing and Services PMI for April. First major data snapshot of how the US economy is absorbing the 15% global tariff. A miss here could accelerate rate-cut expectations and lift risk appetite.

title: US PCE Inflation (March)
desc: The Fed's preferred inflation gauge. Core PCE is the single number that determines whether rate cuts are on or off the table. A soft print is bullish for BTC — it removes one of the last reasons for the Fed to stay hawkish.

title: Bitcoin 2026 Conference — Las Vegas (Apr 27–29)
desc: The world's largest Bitcoin conference returns to The Venetian. Historically a source of major announcements — corporate treasury commitments, protocol updates, sovereign adoption signals. Watch for surprise keynotes.

title: FOMC Decision — Powell's Final Meeting
desc: April 28–29 marks Jerome Powell's last FOMC meeting as Fed Chair before Kevin Warsh takes over on May 15. Rates expected on hold at 3.50–3.75%. The statement and press conference will be watched closely for any shift in tone under incoming hawkish leadership.

title: US GDP Q1 2026 (Advance Estimate)
desc: First reading of Q1 GDP. Tariffs, the Iran shock and Q1 market volatility all hit during this period. The Atlanta Fed GDPNow model has been tracking weakly. A contraction would be a major market-moving event for both equities and crypto.

title: CLARITY Act — Senate Banking Committee Markup Deadline
desc: Late April is the stated window for the Senate Banking Committee markup of the CLARITY Act. If the session is confirmed this week, it signals the most significant crypto regulation vote in US history is imminent. Miss this window and the bill risks falling past the November midterms.
